definition: Calming or quieting language that can bridge toward consummation but is not identical with the governing consummation headwords.
notes: Use this record when the text explicitly names calming or quieting. The compound belongs near the consummation interface, but it should remain distinct from santi, which stays peace-language, and from passaddhi, which is governed as relaxation in practice sequence.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and contemplative contexts
When the text stresses the settling-down or quieting aspect
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

context_rules.context: In compounds or recollection expressions that clearly point toward consummation-side peace
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it. Do not rotate it into the house default.

example_phrases.pali: sabbasaṅkhārasamatho sabbūpadhipaṭinissaggo taṇhakkhayo virāgo nirodho nibbānaṃ
example_phrases.translation: the calming of all constructions, the relinquishment of all acquisitions, the wearing away of ignorant wanting, fading, quenching, nibbāna
example_phrases.notes: Shows upasama-side quieting language in the consummation cluster.
